prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT medium-sized object standing FREE on a small flat patch of pale-blue crystalline ice — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a small tree sheathed in ice 2) a frozen tree encased in glassy ice 3) a faceted crystal column 4) a glowing geode split open 5) a fan of sharp ice needles 6) a cluster of hexagonal crystal columns 7) a crystalline mushroom cluster 8) a tall shard of aquamarine crystal 9) a spiral of clustered icicles 10) a statue encased in blue ice. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
